summary: C5 red phase complete. 11 failing Go tests force the two-call tmux.SendKeys delivery pattern (text with -l flag, pause via injectable Sleeper, then standalone Enter). The previous C4 single-call Enter-invariant tests have been rewritten to inspect the LAST of TWO calls — the spirit is preserved: Enter is still the final thing delivered to the pane. The notify-agent.sh shell script and its test are explicitly out of scope and untouched.
existing_tests_still_green: internal/setup/setup_test.go :: TestNotifyAgentScriptSendKeysLineEndsWithEnter — EXPLICITLY out of scope; do not touch the shell script
All C1/C2/C3 regression tests — still green
TestSendKeys, TestLaunchAgent, TestCLI_MetricsPaneTailsLog, TestCLI_NotifyLogsAndSendsMessage — lenient enough to remain green through the two-call transition

additions: type Sleeper interface { Sleep(d time.Duration) } — injection point, tests pass recording or no-op sleepers
type SendKeysOption func(*sendKeysConfig) — variadic option for SendKeys
type sendKeysConfig struct { sleeper Sleeper; pause time.Duration } — unexported applied config
func WithSleeper(s Sleeper) SendKeysOption — injects a sleeper
func WithPause(d time.Duration) SendKeysOption — sets the inter-call pause
SendKeys signature changed to: func SendKeys(cmd Commander, session, window string, pane int, keys string, opts ...SendKeysOption) error — variadic keeps old callers valid
scaffolding_already_added_to_production.note: These are TYPE DEFINITIONS and a SIGNATURE EXTENSION only — no behavior change. The old one-call body is still there. Your job is to replace the body with the two-call pattern and thread opts through downstream callers.

implementation_punchlist.change: Replace the body of tmux.SendKeys with the two-call pattern. Pseudocode:
pseudocode: if cfg.pause == 0 { cfg.pause = 200 * time.Millisecond } // NOTE: TestSendKeysConfigurablePause asserts pause==0 records 0 sleep — see below
if err := cmd.Run("send-keys", "-t", target, "-l", keys); err != nil { return err }
implementation_punchlist.pause_zero_note: TestSendKeysConfigurablePause iterates over pauses [0, 50ms, 750ms] and asserts the sleeper receives exactly that duration. So if pause==0 is EXPLICITLY passed via WithPause(0), the sleeper MUST still be called once with 0. Distinguish 'not configured' (nil option) from 'configured to 0'. Simplest approach: make pause a *time.Duration in sendKeysConfig, or track a 'configured' bool, or init cfg.pause = -1 and treat -1 as unconfigured. Your choice.

implementation_punchlist.change: Add a realSleeper type (unexported) that wraps time.Sleep:
Extend Notify to accept variadic tmux.SendKeysOption so tests can inject a sleeper. New signature:
pseudocode: func Notify(cmd tmux.Commander, logger *swarmlog.Logger, session string, pane int, message string, opts ...tmux.SendKeysOption) error {
if err := logger.Write(role, message); err != nil { return err }
return tmux.SendKeys(cmd, session, "swarm", pane, message, opts...)
implementation_punchlist.why: Without this, TestNotifyFinalArgIsLiteralEnter burns 200ms of real wall-clock sleep per run. The handoff explicitly forbids real sleep in test paths. Note: the test currently does NOT pass opts — it accepts the 200ms cost for now. If you'd rather the test runs fast, also thread opts through from the acceptance test call site and switch the test to pass tmux.WithSleeper(noop). Either way, the production signature must accept opts.

implementation_punchlist.change: Thread a Sleeper through start.Config so metrics-pane SendKeys can use a no-op in tests. Suggested approach — add fields to start.Config:
pseudocode: Sleeper tmux.Sleeper   // nil => real time.Sleep in metrics init
implementation_punchlist.then: In initMetricsPane, build []tmux.SendKeysOption from cfg.Sleeper / cfg.Pause (skip WithSleeper if cfg.Sleeper == nil, skip WithPause if cfg.Pause == 0) and pass to tmux.SendKeys. In start_test.go's fullCfg helper, set Config.Sleeper to a no-op so TestRunInitsMetricsPaneAsTwoSendKeysCalls (and other start tests) do not sleep.

implementation_punchlist.change: Leave Sleeper / Pause at their zero values so production uses the real time.Sleep + 200ms default. No explicit wiring needed beyond accepting the new Config shape.

TestCLI_NotifyAlwaysSubmitsWithEnter: I did NOT thread opts through this test yet because notify.Notify's current signature does not accept them. Once you extend notify.Notify (step 3), please add `tmux.WithSleeper(noopAcceptanceSleeper{})` to the Notify call in this acceptance test so it does not sleep 200ms. The helper `noopAcceptanceSleeper` is already defined at the bottom of that file.

new_invariant: Across the whole system, after every handoff: the LAST tmux call to the target pane is `send-keys -t <target> Enter` — with Enter as its only non-target argument. The preceding call is `send-keys -t <target> -l <payload>` with the literal-text flag so payloads containing 'Enter' are typed as five characters, not interpreted as the Enter key. A Sleeper runs exactly once between the two calls.
forbidden: Do NOT touch internal/setup/setup.go notify-agent.sh template or its tests — out of scope for C5.
Do NOT remove the existing 'notify-agent.sh helper script submits with Enter' Gherkin scenario or its supporting test.
Do NOT introduce real time.Sleep into any test path. Every test must either inject a no-op/recording sleeper or go through a code path that does.
Do NOT clear the input before sending (no 'C-u' or similar). Do NOT use tmux paste buffers. Do NOT collapse back to a single send-keys call.
how_to_verify: `go test ./...` — the 11 listed red tests must turn green; no previously-green test may regress.
Spot-check timing: `go test -run TestSendKeysSleeperIsInjectable -v ./internal/tmux/` completes in well under 1 second (the test itself asserts this).

rule_3_refactor_carry_forward.why: start.Run had cyclomatic complexity ~7 after C3 (exceeds Constitution's limit of ≤ 4). C5 adds more plumbing for Sleeper/Pause — if you inline it into Run you will make the violation worse.
rule_3_refactor_carry_forward.what_to_do: As part of making C5 green (or immediately after, as a named Refactor step while keeping tests green), split start.Run into a pipeline. Suggested shapes: a []func(Config) error slice executed in order with early-exit on error, or extracted named helpers (runPreflight, runSetup, readConstitution, printBanner, createOrReplaceSession, writeAndLaunchAgents, initMetricsPane, attachToSession). Each helper stays under the complexity cap and Run becomes a short orchestrator.
rule_3_refactor_carry_forward.do_not_drop_this: If you only do C5 and skip the refactor, the Simplicity Enforcer will reject the change. Carry this forward.

constitution_reminders: Rule 1 (TDD): 11 red tests define the contract. Write the minimum production code to turn each green.
Rule 3 (Simplicity): every method stays ≤ 4 cyclomatic complexity and ≤ 30 lines. tmux.SendKeys, notify.Notify, start.Run are the hot spots. Apply the start.Run refactor above.

Rule 4 (Mutation testing): once tests are green, the Mutation Hunter will try to flip operators in the new two-call logic. If a mutant survives (e.g. removing the Sleep call or swapping call order), add a test that kills it.
Rule 5 (Linter): gofmt/go vet clean on every file you touch.
when_you_are_done: Write agent_context/handoff-to-architect.json summarizing what changed (files touched, design choices for Sleeper default and pause-zero semantics, confirmation of the Rule 3 start.Run refactor), then notify pane 0.
